steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence has a main clause ('Anderson chose a narrator who had participated in the protest') followed by a phrase explaining that choice ('a decision that... amplified the agency'). That explanatory phrase is a nonrestrictive appositive — it renames the preceding action. Appositives must be set off by a comma, not a period or semicolon.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'protest a decision that,': No punctuation between 'protest' and 'a decision' runs the appositive into the main clause, creating a run-on. ✗
  • Choice B — 'protest. A decision that': A period creates a sentence fragment — 'A decision that... amplified the agency' has no main verb. ✗
  • Choice C — 'protest; a decision that,': A semicolon links independent clauses; 'a decision that...' is not an independent clause. ✗
  • Choice D — 'protest, a decision that,': A comma correctly introduces the appositive, and the second comma sets off the parenthetical 'by allowing the narrative to be shaped by one of their own.' ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
